stem: For the curve \( [[a]]x^{2}+[[b]]y^{2}=C \) (passing through \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \)), find \( \dfrac{dy}{dx} \) at \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \).
For the curve \( xy=C \), passing through \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \), find \( \dfrac{dy}{dx} \) at \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \).
solution: Differentiating \( xy=C \) implicitly: \( y+x\dfrac{dy}{dx}=0 \), so \( \dfrac{dy}{dx}=-\dfrac{y}{x} = [[answer]] \) at \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the negative sign that comes from moving \( y \) to the other side.
This inverts the ratio of \( y \) to \( x \).
This has the wrong sign AND the inverted ratio.
This multiplies \( x \) and \( y \) instead of dividing.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: For the curve \( [[a]]x^{2}+[[b]]xy+[[c]]y^{2}=C \), passing through \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \), find \( \dfrac{dy}{dx} \) at \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \).
solution: Differentiating implicitly with the product rule on the cross term: \( 2\cdot[[a]]x+[[b]](y+xy')+2\cdot[[c]]yy'=0 \), so \( y'=-\dfrac{2\cdot[[a]]x+[[b]]y}{[[b]]x+2\cdot[[c]]y} \), giving \( [[answer]] \) at \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the cross-term contributions from both the numerator and denominator entirely.
This drops the negative sign from moving terms across the equation.
This drops the cross-term contribution from the denominator only.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: The curve \( [[a]]x + \sin(y) = C \) passes through \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \). Find \( \dfrac{dy}{dx} \) at that point.
solution: Differentiating implicitly: \( [[a]] + \cos(y)\dfrac{dy}{dx}=0 \), so \( \dfrac{dy}{dx}=-\dfrac{[[a]]}{\cos(y)} \), giving \( [[answer]] \) at \( y=[[y0]] \) (note the slope does not depend on \( x_0 \)).
distractor_rationales: This drops the negative sign from moving \( a \) across the equation.
This multiplies by \( \cos(y) \) instead of dividing by it.
This forgets to divide by \( \cos(y) \) entirely.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
